ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively discussed as the original, superior South Korean film that was remade (unauthorized adaptation) into the Bollywood movie 'Zinda'. Praised for its raw narrative, tension, and shocking twist ending, which the remake lacked.

ranked_movies.title: Zinda
ranked_movies.reason: A central topic of discussion as an unauthorized Bollywood adaptation of 'Oldboy'. Received mixed reviews but was noted for its great soundtrack and decent performances, though criticized for failing to capture the essence of the original.
Highly recommended as one of the best queer films ever made in Korea. Praised for its realistic depiction of Korean gay culture and its compelling coming-of-age story about a North Korean defector.

ranked_movies.title: The Negotiation
ranked_movies.reason: Prominently featured and promoted across multiple subreddits as a 'Best Korean Thriller Movie'. Starring Hyun Bin and Son Ye-jin, it was described as a suspenseful, action-packed film about a crisis negotiator.

ranked_movies.title: After Yang
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as the previous film by director Kogonada, which 'made nothing' at the box office, providing context for the discussion around his new project, 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey'.

ranked_movies.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed as an upcoming film with a script by Seth Reiss (co-writer of 'The Menu') and directed by Kogonada. The analysis focused on its box office potential and Sony's significant investment amidst muted marketing.

ranked_movies.title: The Menu
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a previous, successful work by Seth Reiss, who co-wrote the script for the discussed film 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey', adding credibility to the new project.

ranked_movies.title: Mist Whispers Like a Woman
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as one of the films starring the disappeared actress Yoon Young-sil, providing historical context to the unresolved mystery discussed in the thread.

ranked_movies.title: What will we become and where will we meet again
ranked_movies.reason: Another film starring the disappeared actress Yoon Young-sil, cited to establish her career in the 1970s and 80s as part of the mystery's background.

ranked_movies.title: Shootout at Lokhandwala
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned in a comment about the music team 'Strings', who contributed to 'Zinda', also worked on this film, connecting the discussion of soundtracks across Bollywood movies.

ranked_movies.title: Reservoir Dogs
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in a critique of the director of 'Zinda', who was known for copying classics and had previously made a 'terrible version' of this film, adding context to the director's style.

ranked_movies.title: Barbie
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in a box office analysis comment that claimed 'Barbie was a fluke' and that Margot Robbie is not a draw, contrasting the performance of that film with the potential of 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey'.

ranked_movies.title: Caught Stealing
ranked_movies.reason: Used as a box office comp in the analysis for 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey', with its poor ticket sales cited to illustrate the potential underperformance of the discussed film.

ranked_movies.title: The Roses
ranked_movies.reason: Another film used as a box office comparison point in the analysis for 'A Big Bold Beautiful Journey', highlighting the soft pre-sales and marketing push for the latter.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy (Hollywood Remake)
ranked_movies.reason: Briefly mentioned as being 'even worse than Zinda', providing a point of comparison for the quality of international remakes of the original Korean classic.
